Off Calendar Oct-22-2018 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-04-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Love, Esq. of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for Defendant Olympic Glove.
Asbestos Order to Show Cause.

The Court stated that counsel failed to comply with the Court's May 31, 2018 preference order, and that counsel had failed to provide the confidential settlement demands as set forth in the May 31, 2018 order. The Court reads the portion of the May 31, 2018 order with regard to the confidential settlement demands in open court. The Court addressed the non-appearance by Mr. Love at the September 17, 2018 OSC hearing.

Mr. Love addressed his non-appearance stating that the email from Ms. Ly was not specific to him and that he sent the appropriate person to the hearing. In addition, Mr. Love stated that the information from the orders weren't making it in to their system/to the "case handling attorney's",that his office held a meeting to correct the problem, that going forward they are making sure that the dates are on their calendar, and that they have been complying going forward.

At the conclusion of Mr. Love's comments, the Court admonished Mr. Love stating he was at the meeting with Judge Jackson regarding expediting the cases and that the system won't work if there isn't compliance with the settlement orders. In addition, the Court stated that it finds that there is a lack of sincerity, that it will give this process until the end of the year, and if there isn't compliance then it may reinstate the old process. The Court stated that there was a modification of the mandatory settlement conferences and the batching of cases. In conclusion, the Court stated that if the parties aren't going to help Commissioner Ly out then why would it continue with the new system.

The Court stated that any further non-compliance with this order or any Court order would result in the following consequences: sanctions and/or being reported to the State Bar by the Court.

Order to Show Cause discharged as to Defendant Olympic Glove.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-03-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Carol Nicola

RULING:
DEFENDANT RESEARCH-COTTRELL, INC. N/K/A AWT AIR COMPANY INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Research-Cottrell, Inc.'s motion to strike plaintiff's complaint is denied. Even assuming that at the time she filed her complaint plaintiff was not ignorant of defendant's identity or facts giving rise to its liability, based on the evidence before the Court the statute of limitations has not run. (C.C.P. 340.2.) Instead of filing a Doe amendment plaintiff could have filed an amended complaint naming Research-Cottrell, Inc. as a defendant. Therefore this Court treats plaintiff's Doe amendment the same way it would treat an amended complaint. (Davis v. Marin (2000) 80 Cal.App.4th 380.) Such an amended complaint would be proper because the statute of limitations has not run. Additionally, defendant did not demonstrate unreasonable delay that resulted in specific prejudice to defendant. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted in its entirety. While the motion is titled a "motion to strike," defendant does not move pursuant to C.C.P. sections 435-437. The Court focuses on the substance of the motion and considers it an evidence-based motion. (See A.N. v. County of Los Angeles (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1058; Barrows v. American Motors Corp. (1983) 144 Cal.App.3d 1.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Lynn O'Malley-Taylor, Matter not reported. (503/LOT)
